status_message: Your recovery is moderate—today is perfect for a focused strength session after 6pm to build that six-pack foundation!
weekly_insight: Week 1 baseline established. No workouts logged yet. Starting fresh with excellent gym availability: target 4 sessions this week (Mon/Tue/Wed/Thu).
monthly_insight: You're on Day 1 of your six-pack journey. Consistency over the next 4 weeks will build momentum. Focus on progressive overload and maintaining your 1800 calorie target to reveal definition.

workout.rationale: After 6pm gym slot available. Moderate recovery allows controlled strength work. Prioritize core and upper body to build six-pack muscles while maintaining fat loss deficit.
workout.warmup: 5 min light cardio + arm circles + cat-cow stretches
workout.cooldown: 5 min walking + static stretches (chest, shoulders, core)

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Control the descent, squeeze at top for 1 second
exercises.youtube_search: dumbbell bench press proper form beginner

exercises.name: Bent-Over Barbell Rows
exercises.notes: Keep chest up, row to lower ribs
exercises.youtube_search: bent over barbell rows proper form

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raises
exercises.notes: Control the swing, engage core at top

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Flyes
exercises.notes: Slight bend in elbows, chest stretch at bottom

exercises.name: Dead bugs
exercises.notes: Move slowly, never let lower back lift off floor

daily_tips: Drink 3-4 liters of water today—hydration improves recovery and reduces bloating for visible abs
Your resting HR of 76 is good; focus on steady sleep tonight to boost tomorrow's recovery score above 70%
Hit the after-6pm gym session: compound lifts + core work will maximize your calorie deficit without overtraining
progress_insight: You're starting strong with a structured non-veg diet and clear gym schedule. Your 1800 calorie target creates a 300-500 calorie daily deficit, ideal for fat loss. The six-pack requires 3 elements: progressive strength training (building the rectus abdominis and obliques), consistent calorie deficit (revealing the muscles), and core-focused work. At 70kg and 163cm, you're roughly 5-8kg away from visible abs if body fat drops to 12-14%. Your WHOOP data shows you're well-rested—capitalize this week with 4 focused workouts combining compound lifts and dedicated core. Consistency beats perfection; nail your meal plan and gym sessions Monday through Thursday this week.
